age_3.text:
Age 3. Grandma's Living Room.
The carpet is scratchy under your bare feet. Grandma sits in her armchair, her hands warm as she holds your tiny wrists. The afternoon sun filters through the lace curtains.
"Ainsi font, font, font..." she sings, her voice raspy but gentle.
She moves your hands in rhythm. Left. Right. Twirl.
"Les petites marionnettes..."
You are only three years old, but you remember this moment with terrifying clarity. It was the first time you felt the music.
What did you feel in that moment?

choices.text: I felt like the center of the universe. I loved the attention.
Her voice enchanted me. I tried to mimic every pitch and tone.
My body moved on its own. I had to dance.

age_3_end.text:
The memory fades. Grandma is gone.
Only the cold silence of your parents' house remains.

age_5.text:
Age 5. Friday Night.
The TV is blaring the evening news. Your father is slumped on the sofa, exhausted from work. Your mother is in the kitchen, stress radiating off her like heat.
You climb onto the low coffee table. This is your stage. You hold a wooden spoon like a microphone.
"Look at me!" you scream. "Look at me!"
You start to perform the dance you saw on the cartoon show. You give it everything you have.
How did your parents react?

choices.text: Mom looked up. She didn't smile. "Your leg is crooked."
choices.resultText: You learned right then: Love is a reward for perfection.

choices.text: Dad sighed. "Get down. You're blocking the news."
choices.resultText: You learned right then: I have to scream to be heard.

choices.text: They ignored me completely. So I closed my eyes and danced for myself.
choices.resultText:
You were alone in the room, even with them there. But you felt like royalty.
        You learned right then: The stage is the only safe place.

age_7.text:
Age 7. The Elementary School Gym.
It smells of rubber mats and sweat. Parents line the back wall with camcorders.
The kids in your class always teased you, but now, the room is quiet. It's your turn.
What did you do on that stage?

choices.text: I sang a sad ballad. I let all the loneliness in the house spill out.
choices.resultText: When you finished, there was total silence. Then, you saw the music teacher wiping a tear. Your power is emotion.

choices.text: I danced a pop routine with military precision. I was a machine.
choices.resultText: You didn't miss a single beat. You felt total control over your body.

choices.text: I just stood there, winked, and posed. The charisma did the work.
choices.resultText: The parents clapped louder for you than anyone else. The other kids stared in jealousy. You realized you had "It."

age_9.text:
Age 9. Your Bedroom.
Outside your door, the fighting has started again. Shouting about money. The sound of a plate shattering.
You crawl under your duvet cover with an old MP3 player and big headphones.
You press PLAY. The world disappears.
What are you watching to survive the night?

choices.text: A video of a perfect Idol group. Everyone is smiling, beautiful, and rich.
choices.resultText: You don't just want to sing. You want their lives. You want to escape the ugliness of your reality into their fantasy.

choices.text: A raw, acoustic track about pain and survival.
choices.resultText: You realize you aren't alone. If they can turn pain into art, so can you.

choices.text: A hardcore dance practice video. The sweat, the synchronization, the order.
choices.resultText: Out there, it's chaos. In here, there are rules. If you move exactly on the beat, nothing bad can hurt you.

age_10.text:
Age 10. The Community Center.
You begged your mother to pay for lessons. The mirrors are cracked.
The instructor grabs your shoulder after class, a little too roughly.
"You have talent" he says. "But in Seoul, talent is cheap. Are you ready to bleed for this?"

choices.text: "I will sleep in the studio if I have to. I won't get tired."
choices.resultText: He nods. "We'll see how long you last."

choices.text: "Just tell me what to do, Teacher. I will be perfect."
choices.resultText: He sighs. "Good. The agencies like soldiers."

choices.text: "I'm going to be so beautiful they won't be able to say no."
choices.resultText: He chuckles darkly. "Beauty fades. Work on your basics."

age_11.text:
Age 11. The Kitchen Table.
You sit your parents down. The community center isn't enough anymore. You need a real coach to get to the next level.
Your father looks at the glossy brochure for the private academy. He taps his finger on the price tag.
"Do you know how many shifts I have to work to pay for this?" he asks, his voice heavy.
Your mother crosses her arms. "If we pay for this, you cannot quit. Ever. You treat this like a job."
How do you convince them?

choices.text: "I won't quit. I'll work harder than anyone else." (Focus: Dance)
choices.resultText:
Your father sighs and opens his wallet. "This is your inheritance," he grumbles. "Don't waste it."
        Your mother nods approvingly. She likes the ambition, even if it costs them.

choices.text: "I need this. My voice is my future." (Focus: Vocals)
choices.resultText:
"It better be," your father mutters, signing the check with a frown.
        Your mother smooths your hair. "Then make us proud. No mistakes."

choices.text: "Think of it as an investment. I'll pay you back tenfold." (Focus: Boldness)
choices.resultText:
Your father stops writing. He looks at you, surprised, then lets out a dry laugh.
        "Big words," he says, closing the checkbook but looking impressed. "Okay. I like that attitude. Make me rich, kid."
        Your mother frowns. "It's not about money. It's about discipline."

age_12.text:
Age 12. Hongdae Street.
You dragged a small speaker to the busy shopping district.
Suddenly the speaker dies. The music cuts out in the middle of the chorus.
Panic rises in your throat. What did you do?

choices.text: I froze. I grabbed my bag and ran home, crying.
choices.resultText: The shame burned for weeks. It planted a seed of stage fright deep in your chest.

choices.text: I kept singing a cappella, louder than before.
choices.resultText: The crowd cheered for your bravery. You learned you are stronger than your fear.

choices.text: I laughed, made a joke about "cheap equipment," and charmed the crowd.
choices.resultText: You made them smile. You learned that personality can save you when talent fails.
